title: Agentic AI’s Growing Pains
text:
Agentic AI’s Growing Pains

Huang Shichan

Gavekal Technologies: Chips | 25 Jun 2026

Beijing is all-in on AI agents: its AI-Plus strategy calls for agents to be nearly ubiquitous in the economy by 2030. And agents are proliferating, for both consumer and business use. Enterprises will be the main driver of agentic growth: consumers have begun to sour on standalone agents such as OpenClaw in favor of AI functions embedded in other applications. The enterprise agent market is estimated to double this year to RMB45bn, and to maintain double- or triple-digit growth rates for the next several years. But widespread adoption still faces many hurdles: agent reliability is still spotty, training data is hard to access and to standardize, and general agent-builders such as Alibaba are short on the domain expertise needed to build industry-specific agents.
